questions.q: What is the primary function of a stack in computer science?
questions.answer: The primary function of a stack is to follow the LIFO (Last In First Out) principle, where the last element added to the stack is the first one to be removed.

questions.q: Which of the following operations is NOT a basic operation that can be performed on a stack?
Explain the concept of stack overflow with an example.
questions.answer: Stack overflow occurs when a program attempts to use more memory than is available on the call stack. For example, if a function calls itself recursively without a base case, it can lead to a stack overflow error.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the POP operation in a stack?
questions.answer: The purpose of the POP operation in a stack is to remove the top element from the stack.

questions.q: True or False: A stack can be implemented using a linked list.
What is the difference between a stack and a queue?
questions.answer: A stack follows the LIFO principle, whereas a queue follows the FIFO principle.

questions.q: Which of the following is an application of stacks?
Explain the concept of stack underflow with an example.
questions.answer: Stack underflow occurs when a program attempts to remove an element from an empty stack. For example, if a function attempts to pop an element from an empty stack, it can lead to a stack underflow error.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the PUSH operation in a stack?
questions.answer: The purpose of the PUSH operation in a stack is to add a new element to the top of the stack.

questions.q: True or False: A stack is a type of tree data structure.
